title: BOCW Licence Registration Online | Apply BOCW Registration

hero.headline: BOCW Registration for Construction Businesses
hero.subtext: Fast, accurate and compliant registration under BOCW Act with Professionals Law’s expert support.

mainHeading: BOCW Licence Registration Online – Apply BOCW Registration in India

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: BOCW Registration is compulsory for the entities which are involved in construction activities with more than 10 workers under the Building and Other Construction Workers (Regulation of Employment and Conditions of Service) Act, 1996. It is intended to protect the rights, health, safety and welfare of construction workers. Employers will have to register their establishment and make contribution to the welfare fund maintained by the State Welfare Boards. Professionals Law helps construction companies, developers and contractors to obtain BOCW registration and comply with local labour laws in each state.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: BOCW Registration is a requirement of law for legitimate identity of construction businesses and for workers to claim benefits of welfare like health care, education, maternity, accident coverage and pension. Non-registration shall be liable to penal provisions and delays in completion of the project. Professionals Law guarantees the registration to be an easy, compliance task with the reduced regulatory risk and keeping the best interest of your employees.

body.title: Legal Compliance
body.description: BOCW Registration ensures the employer operates within the legal framework, avoiding penalties and fulfilling obligations under labour laws.

body.title: Access to Welfare Fund
body.description: Registered establishments enable workers to access benefits like accident coverage, educational assistance and health insurance through the State Welfare Board.

body.title: Increased Worker Morale
body.description: With access to welfare schemes and social security benefits, workers feel more valued and secure, which boosts productivity and retention.

body.title: Government Project Eligibility
body.description: Registration is often mandatory for participating in government-funded infrastructure or public works projects.

body.title: Avoidance of Legal Action
body.description: Non-registration can lead to penalties, stoppage of work and inspections, which are avoided through timely compliance.

criteria: Establishments involved in building or construction work.
Must employ 10 or more construction workers on any day during the preceding 12 months.
Applicable to both private and public sector contractors, builders and developers.
Includes activities like building, roadwork, irrigation, demolition and other construction-related activities.

documents: Address proof of the site (rent agreement/ownership proof)
Proof of ownership or lease deed of construction site
Identity proof and photographs of employers/owners
List of workers employed with their details
Copy of contract or work order

body.description: Professionals Law assesses the nature and scale of construction activity to determine applicability and documentation requirements.
All necessary documents and worker information are compiled and verified.
Online or offline submission is made with the respective State Welfare Board or Labour Department.
Some states may conduct an on-site inspection or request additional clarification.
Upon approval, the BOCW Registration certificate is granted and the establishment becomes eligible for compliance and welfare contributions.

penalties.heading: Penalties
body: Failure to register may result in a fine up to ₹1,000 per day during the period of default.
Repeated violations may attract further penalties, including work stoppages, blacklisting and prosecution.
Employers may also be barred from availing welfare board benefits for their workers if registration is not obtained in time.

body.title: Standard Registration Timeline
body.description: The BOCW registration process typically takes 7 to 15 working days, depending on the state and completeness of the documentation.

body.question: What is BOCW Registration?
body.answer: BOCW Registration is a legal requirement for establishments engaged in construction activities with 10 or more workers under the BOCW Act, 1996.

body.question: Who needs BOCW Registration?
body.answer: Any builder, contractor or developer employing 10 or more construction workers for projects like buildings, roads, dams or infrastructure work must register.

body.question: Is BOCW applicable to renovation projects?
body.answer: Yes, if the renovation involves construction work and employs 10 or more workers, BOCW registration is required.

body.question: How long is the registration valid?
body.answer: Validity depends on the duration of the project or as defined by the state’s BOCW Welfare Board, usually for the life of the project.

body.question: Do workers get benefits through BOCW registration?
body.answer: Yes, workers are eligible for several welfare schemes, including pensions, accident cover, maternity benefits and education support.

body.question: Is there a registration fee?
body.answer: Yes, a nominal registration fee must be paid based on the size and value of the construction project. Professionals Law guides you on state-specific fee structures.

body.question: Is online registration available?
body.answer: Yes, most states offer online registration portals. Professionals Law ensures smooth and error-free online submission.

body.question: What happens if I don’t register?
body.answer: Non-compliance can result in penalties, work stoppages and being ineligible for government contracts or project approvals.

body.question: Can Professionals Law help with ongoing compliance after registration?
body.answer: Absolutely. Professionals Law offers end-to-end compliance management, including labour returns, renewal tracking and coordination with welfare boards.
